en-US.building-variant.arctic-cutter.title: Cutter
en-US.building-variant.asteroid-stabilizer.des: <gl>Stabilizes</gl> an asteroid and pushes away the storm by storing shapes in the asteroid. Incorrect shapes are destroyed.
en-US.building-variant.layer-detacher.title: Layer Detacher
en-US.building-variant.layer-detacher.descript: <gl>Detaches</gl> the <gl>top layer</gl> of a shape and puts it on a separate belt
en-US.building-variant.communication-relay.des: Use the communication relay to declare which <gl>shapes</gl> you want to receive through the <gl>vortex</gl>
en-US.building-variant.data-fragment.title: Data Fragment
en-US.building-variant.data-fragment.descripti: Contains a preserved piece of technology from a world consumed by never-ending winter
en-US.building-variant.ruin-wall.title: Ruin Wall
en-US.building-variant.ruin-wall.description:
Once providing protection from the elements,
now just a casualty from a war without ammunition
en-US.building-variant.ruin-rubble.title: Rubble
en-US.building-variant.ruin-rubble.description: There used to be some kind of building here, impossible to tell its former function...
en-US.game-mode.ArcticRuins.title: Arctic Ruins
en-US.game-mode.ArcticRuins.description: Critical: We just returned from summer break, went to boot the factory back up, and the vortex stopped accepting shapes!! We're sending you, totally qualified employee, to investigate
en-US.scenario.arctic-ruins.title: The Other Side
en-US.scenario.arctic-ruins.description: It's clear that something is wrong with wherever the vortex is sending shapes to. Since you are assigned to deal with the problem, you are put in a rocket and <gl>send through the vortex</gl> (which is perfectly safe maybe) to find out what's happening on <gl>the other side</gl>
en-US.scenario.artic-ruins.intro.vortex:
Alright, we're <gl>sending you through the vortex</gl> now, tell us what you find on the other side.
Oh! And one last thing: <gl>Do. Not. Crash. The Rocket!</gl> It is your one way back, ok?
en-US.scenario.artic-ruins.intro.tutorial:
Great... do you know how much these things cost?! I claim no responsibility in this!
Luckily, it sounds like the communication relay in there is still working. So you can still use it to tell us which <gl>shapes</gl> we should send through the <gl>vortex</gl>.
You'll have to figure out something to do with them, did you say you can see remains of a factory around you? Maybe you can <gl>find</gl> some new buildings in the area around you to replace the ones that got caught up in your... "landing" - Well, good luck!
en-US.scenario-preset.artic-ruins.title: Arctic Ruins Preset
en-US.scenario-preset.artic-ruins.description: The preset for the Arctic Ruins game mode. Be careful modifying it, since the game mode is made for this specific set of configurations (mostly make sure that it's not painting shapes before you have access to the painter). Also note that in contrast to the base game, more asteroids will make it more difficult, since you have to complete more of them
en-US.player-badge.arctic-ruins.title: Freezing Disassembly
en-US.ui.arctic-ruins.shapes-unlocked.Mileston:
You might be wondering what even the point is in us assembling the shapes and then sending them over, just so you can disassemble them again. But I can assure you that we're still increasing the GVP (Gross Vortexic Product), which is obviously the only thing that really matters. Luckily, it's not even negatively affected by how you crashed your only way back, otherwise we would have to actually take action

Anyway, we've got a new shape ready, you can select it at the communication relay

Did you know you're the reason we had to change our "Days since last incident" sign? Some intern had to add "On this side of the vortex" with a sharpie, which is kinda making us look silly.

Be that as it may, there's another new shape, see if you can do something with it

By the way, if you're wondering who came up with the idea to attach the radio dish to the side of the rocket (The one you crashed), where it would be the first thing to burn up if you had entered an atmosphere, yeah I don't know either. Probably some tired mod developer who thought the flat design was too boring and this was the best thing that came to mind

On the plus side, you can now go to the communication relay and look at the new shape we made available, just for you

Heyy, you wanna come over and tell me what you think of my GLaDOS cosplay? Well, too bad, you crashed your ship!!

Anyway, new shape is available

And just so you know, everybody at the convention loved my cosplay, and you're not gonna get to see it now. So, who would you cosplay as? The Martian?

Also, there's a new shape available at the communication relay

Eyy, you did it! Thank you for exploring all that space for us, as a reward, we're gonna give you this shiny badge, isn't that cool? What more could you want in life? (Except maybe a working rocket)

<info>But for real, thank you for playing through my mod, I hope you enjoyed it</info> :)
en-US.research.Milestone_InitialArticRuins.des: After you very gracefully absolutely smashed your ship into the ground, you still managed to salvage a few basic building types. The rest you'll have to find again
en-US.research.Milestone_BasicDisassemblyArtic: All the necessary tools to disassemble shapes from the vortex
en-US.research.Milestone_AdvancedMachiningArti: Let your machines cuddle up with even more compact factories
